text.title: multi-agent-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for multi-agent-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Role confusion — overlapping agent responsibilities cause agents to duplicate work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If two agents have similar system prompts and responsibilities, they produce redundant outputs, wasting tokens and confusing the orchestrator.

failure-mode.description: Communication overhead — every send/receive consumes tokens; small tasks pay disproportionate coordination tax.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-agent systems have setup cost. For simple problems, direct single-agent approach is cheaper.

failure-mode.description: Deadlocks — agents waiting on each other's outputs without timeout hang indefinitely.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If Agent A waits for B and B waits for A, the system deadlocks. Use a coordinator that detects cycles at plan-time.

failure-mode.description: Echo chambers — agents with similar training data converge on the same (possibly wrong) answer in debate or review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Large language models trained on similar data produce correlated outputs. Debate is less useful if both sides reach the same incorrect conclusion.

failure-mode.description: Coordination failure — without a clear decision-making authority, no consensus is reached.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Specify upfront who decides: the orchestrator, the judge, majority vote, or consensus.

failure-mode.description: Resource waste — parallel agents sharing a rate-limited API key hit limits faster.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 5 parallel agents hitting the same key multiply effective request rate by 5. Use per-agent rate limiters or a shared token bucket.

failure-mode.description: Sequential bottlenecks — debate and collaborative patterns serialize naturally; parallelism gains are limited.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Not all patterns parallelize equally. Debate requires sequential rounds; only independent stages can run in parallel.
